sections.title: Regulatory Framework — Quick Reference
sections.contentHtml:
The table below defines the governing framework for each compliance area. Use this as the authoritative reference when determining which law applies to a specific platform decision.

Compliance AreaGoverning Authority
Platform ObligationMLS feed access and display rules
Stellar MLS via PRAR / NAR IDX Policy 7.58Execute IDX/VOW agreements. Apply all display, attribution, purge, and compensation filter rules.
Agent license verificationJECVEBR — PR Examining Board / P.R. Laws Ann. tit. 20 Ch. 99A
Verify active license + $10,000 bond before granting listing access.Listing status and CCP compliance
Stellar MLS Rules / NAR CCP Policy 8.0Monitor public marketing triggers. Enforce 5 business day Stellar MLS PR entry window.
FinCEN AML screeningFinCEN RRE Rule — effective March 1, 2026
Screen and flag entity/trust buyers in non-financed transactions. Notario files — platform flags.Fair Housing — federal classes
Fair Housing Act (FHA) / HUD 2023 AI GuidanceAudit ranking algorithms for disparate impact. No steering on 7 federal protected classes.
Fair Housing — PR additional classesPuerto Rico civil rights laws
Extend algorithm audits to: political ideology, social condition, sexual orientation, source of income.ADA web accessibility
Title III ADA / WCAG 2.1 Level AAFull accessibility in English and Spanish. Annual screen-reader audit.
Email marketingCAN-SPAM Act
Opt-out model. Unsubscribe within 10 business days. $53,088/email violation penalty.STR listing compliance
PRTC (Tourism Company) / Act 272-2003Verify PRTC Innkeeper ID before publishing STR listing. Disclose 7% room tax.
Data privacyFTC Act Section 5 + state laws for out-of-PR users
No PR-specific privacy statute. Apply CCPA/VCDPA etc. for users in those states.Sold price disclosure
Puerto Rico is full disclosure jurisdictionNo non-disclosure restrictions. Sold prices available via VOW without limitation.
iMapp / Data Co-op toolsStellar MLS rules
These tools are for Florida properties only. Must be blocked for all PR-address listings.

sections.title: Agent License Verification — JECVEBR
sections.contentHtml:
Before granting any agent or agency access to publish listings on the platform, verify that they hold a valid, active JECVEBR license and a current $10,000 surety bond. This is the platform's onboarding gate — the license is the minimum legal credential to mediate property transactions in Puerto Rico.

How to Verify

Verification MethodURL / Contact
What to CheckPCS License Verification Portal
prbienesyraices@pcshq.com / 1-888-773-9266Active status, license type, expiry date
JECVEBR Online Validation Toolpuertoricorealtor.org
Cross-reference license number against JECVEBR registryKARIBE Property Registry
puertoricorealtor.org → KARIBEUse for property-level title and lien checks, not agent verification

Valid License Types

License TypeSpanish Name
Platform ActionReal Estate Broker
Corredor de Bienes RaícesOK to onboard. Verify active status + $10,000 bond current.
Real Estate SalespersonVendedor de Bienes Raíces
OK to onboard under sponsoring broker. Verify active status + $10,000 bond + sponsoring broker license.Real Estate Company
Empresa de Bienes RaícesOK to onboard. Verify company license active. Check that individual agents under company also hold personal licenses.
No JECVEBR licenseN/A
DO NOT ONBOARD. Reject listing submission. Private individuals selling own property are exempt from licensing but may not use platform in a commercial mediation capacity.BOND REQUIREMENT: Puerto Rico uniquely requires a $10,000 surety bond (fianza) from all licensed brokers and salespersons. A valid license without a current bond is non-compliant. Verify bond status as part of onboarding — confirm the bond is active and covers the current date.

________________

sections.title: Stellar MLS Feed Rules — IDX, VOW, CCP, DMEL
sections.contentHtml:
Puerto Rico's MLS is operated by Stellar MLS in partnership with PRAR. The platform's IDX and VOW access comes through this relationship. All Stellar MLS rules that apply to Florida listings apply identically to Puerto Rico listings. The MLS platform is Matrix. Feed contact: PRAR@stellarmls.com / 800-686-7451 option 4.

IDX Display Rules — Mandatory

Display ElementRule
ImplementationBroker attribution
Listing firm name + phone OR email on every PDP and thumbnailMust be 'reasonably prominent.' Stellar MLS runs automated attribution audits remotely.
Buyer broker compensationPROHIBITED — absolute ban
Strip from ALL fields: remarks, notes, agent comments, any free-text field. Post-Sitzer/Burnett mandate.BuyerAgencyCompensation RESO field
DEPRECATED — treat as nullRemoved from RESO Data Dictionary. Do not ingest, store, or display.
Seller concessionsPermitted — designated concession fields only
Source only from MLS concession fields. Never from remark or note fields.Photo watermarks
Must be preservedNo cropping, overlaying, or removal. Stellar MLS enforces with fines.
Expired listingsPurge within 24 hours
Automate removal. Same rule as Florida.Withdrawn listings
Purge within 24 hoursAutomate removal. Same rule as Florida.
Co-minglingMLS data and non-MLS data must be labeled or shown separately
Verify current Stellar MLS PR IDX agreement for 2026 co-mingling status.iMapp and Data Co-op
BLOCKED for Puerto Rico listingsThese tools apply to Florida properties only. Must not be surfaced for any PR-address listing.
Feed deliveryRESO Web API — OData v4, OAuth 2.0, TLS 1.2+, JSON
RETS fully deprecated. Reject any feed still on RETS. 

VOW Rules

* Registration wall: full legal name + valid email address required before accessing VOW data.

* Email verification: confirmation email sent and verified before access granted — no exceptions.

* Terms of Use: consumer must affirmatively accept TOU restricting data to personal non-commercial use.

* Redistribution: VOW data may not be redistributed or retransmitted by the consumer.

* Audit logs: maintain logs of all VOW registrations and listings viewed per user. Required for Stellar MLS compliance reviews.

* Sold price data: Puerto Rico is a full disclosure jurisdiction — sold prices available via VOW without restriction. No non-disclosure flag applies to any PR listing.

Clear Cooperation Policy — Puerto Rico Specific Rule

PR-SPECIFIC WINDOW: Stellar MLS applies NAR's Clear Cooperation Policy 8.0 to Puerto Rico with a five (5) business day entry window — not the standard one business day. An agent has 5 business days from signing a listing agreement to enter the listing into Matrix, OR file a Temporary Exclusion or Office Exclusive form. If any public marketing occurs during this window, CCP is triggered and the listing must be entered into the MLS within one business day of that marketing event.

Public marketing triggers — any of the following activates the one business day MLS entry requirement:

* Yard signs or any physical marketing materials

* Any digital marketing on a public website, including social media posts

* Emails sent to more than one outside brokerage

* One-to-one broker communications do NOT trigger CCP

Zillow Listing Access Standards — Applies to Puerto Rico

* Zillow's LAS requires any publicly marketed property to appear in Stellar MLS within one business day to be eligible for Zillow display.

* If a Puerto Rico listing is publicly marketed for more than 24 hours before MLS entry, Zillow may ban that listing for the full duration of the listing agreement.

* Zillow Preview (announced March 17, 2026 — live April 2026): pre-market listings publicly displayed on Zillow/Trulia before MLS entry. Will include Puerto Rico listings. Platforms must handle the new Preview listing status type in feed ingestion logic.

* Platform obligation: surface LAS compliance status for all PR listings. Flag any listing that has been publicly marketed without corresponding Stellar MLS entry.

DMEL — Delayed Marketing Exempt Listing

Stellar MLS implemented the DMEL category on September 16, 2025, applying identically to Florida and Puerto Rico.

* Sellers must sign the Owners Exclusion Form before DMEL status can be applied.

* The signed Owners Exclusion Form must be uploaded within 24 hours of any MLS request.

* During the delay period: listing visible to MLS participants and registered VOW clients only. Does NOT appear on public IDX portals.

* After delay period: immediately eligible for full IDX and VOW distribution.

Listing Status Reference

StatusIDX Eligible
VOW EligiblePlatform Action
ActiveYES
YESStandard distribution. No special action.
Coming SoonNO (local option)
YESCheck Stellar MLS Puerto Rico rules for current time limits.
DMELNO
YESVerify Owners Exclusion Form on file and uploaded within 24hr.
Office ExclusiveNO
NOInternal use only. No public display on platform.
PendingLocal option
YESVerify Stellar MLS per-feed settings before displaying.
ExpiredNO
NOPurge within 24 hours. Automate.
WithdrawnNO
NOPurge within 24 hours. Automate.

New Member Training Gate

* All new Stellar MLS participants must complete required training within 60 days of joining.

* Platform obligation: agents who have not completed training CANNOT add or edit listings in Matrix. Do not accept listing submissions from agents flagged as training-incomplete.

* Training available in both English and Spanish.

NAR 2026 Policy Changes Affecting Feed Rules

PolicyChange
Platform ImpactPS 7.87 — Syndication to Aggregators
REPEALED — no national frameworkNo national syndication right exists. Every portal/aggregator deal requires a per-MLS agreement with Stellar MLS / PRAR.
PS 7.58 — IDX Limited to REALTORS® OnlyAMENDED — restriction removed
IDX display rights no longer limited to REALTOR® members. Broader eligibility is now the default.PS 7.89 — $15,000 Fine Cap
REPEALEDStellar MLS now sets its own fine schedule with no national ceiling. Platform violations may carry higher penalties.
PS 7.62 — Open Listings ExcludedREPEALED
Stellar MLS may now accept open listings where PR law permits. Update listing type logic.NAR Self-Cert Deadline
March 1, 2026Verify Stellar MLS / PRAR has self-certified 2026 Handbook adoption. Flag if not confirmed.

sections.title: FinCEN RRE Rule — Platform Screening Obligation
sections.contentHtml:
The FinCEN Residential Real Estate Rule (effective March 1, 2026) applies to Puerto Rico in full — all 78 municipalities are covered with no geographic limitations and no purchase price threshold. The platform's obligation is to screen and flag qualifying transactions. The notario (closing attorney) is the reporting person who files — not the platform.

Screening Trigger — Three Conditions

A FinCEN report is required when ALL three conditions are met simultaneously:

ConditionDefinition
Platform Screening Action1. Residential real estate transferred
Any residential property sale or transfer in Puerto RicoDefault: all PR residential listings are in scope.
2. Buyer is a legal entity or trustLLC, corporation, partnership, trust — NOT an individual taking title in their own name
Collect buyer entity type at listing intake / transaction stage. Flag all non-individual buyers.3. Transaction is non-financed
No mortgage from a bank/lender subject to AML obligations. Includes: all-cash, seller-financed, privately financed.Ask buyers for financing type. Flag all non-financed transactions.
PR-SPECIFIC NOTE: Puerto Rico uses a civil law notary system — all deeds are executed before a Notario Público who is a licensed attorney. In most Puerto Rico closings, the notario is the designated reporting person under the FinCEN cascade. Inform developer and agency partners: if a buyer is an entity or trust paying cash, the notario must file before closing. 

Platform Workflow

* At listing intake: collect and store whether seller is individual or entity — relevant for downstream transaction screening.

* At transaction/offer stage: collect buyer entity type (individual vs. entity/trust) and financing method (financed vs. non-financed).

* Flag qualifying transactions: if buyer = entity/trust AND financing = non-financed → set FinCEN RRE flag = TRUE.

* Notify the agent and notario: surface the flag clearly in the platform UI so the closing attorney is alerted before the closing date.

* Do not file the report: that is the notario's obligation. The platform screens and flags only.

* Record retention: log all transactions screened and the outcome of the screening for 5 years.

sections.title: Short-Term Rental Listing Compliance
sections.contentHtml:
Short-term rental (STR) listings require additional verification and disclosure steps before the platform may publish them. The platform's obligations are limited to: verifying the operator's PRTC registration, displaying the registration ID, and disclosing the 7% room tax to prospective guests.

What Qualifies as a Short-Term Rental

* Any rental of fewer than 90 consecutive days qualifies as an STR (hospedaje temporero) under Puerto Rico law.

* Long-term rentals (90+ days) are treated as standard residential rental listings — no STR-specific compliance steps apply.

* Platform obligation: collect rental duration type at listing intake. Apply STR compliance workflow only to listings offering sub-90-day rentals.

PRTC Registration Verification

RequirementDetail
Platform ActionPRTC Innkeeper ID
All STR operators must register with Puerto Rico Tourism Company (PRTC) under Act 272-2003 before accepting guestsCollect PRTC Innkeeper ID at STR listing onboarding. Do not publish listing without confirmed ID.
Display on listingInnkeeper ID must be visible on the STR listing page
Surface PRTC ID in listing display. Include in listing data schema.Verification method
Check against PRTC registry or request operator to provide registration certificateStore ID in platform database. Flag unverified IDs for manual review.
Municipal licenseEach of PR's 78 municipalities requires a separate business license
Collect municipality name. Note that municipal license is operator's responsibility — platform verifies PRTC ID only. 

Room Occupancy Tax — 7% Disclosure

* Rate: 7% of listing price including cleaning fees. Applies to all stays of 89 nights or fewer.

* Airbnb: automatically collects and remits 7% PR Room Tax on behalf of hosts — no host action required for Airbnb bookings.

* All other OTAs and direct bookings: host is responsible for collecting from guest and remitting monthly to PRTC.

* Platform obligation: disclose the 7% room tax on all STR listings. If the platform processes direct bookings (not via Airbnb), surface the host's remittance obligation clearly in the operator dashboard.

DISCLOSURE LANGUAGE: STR listings must clearly state: 'A 7% Puerto Rico Room Occupancy Tax applies to all reservations of 89 nights or fewer. This tax is collected separately.' Platforms routing bookings through Airbnb may note that Airbnb collects this tax automatically. For all other booking methods, disclose that the host is responsible for collection and remittance.

sections.title: Fair Housing — Algorithm and Display Obligations
sections.contentHtml:
The Fair Housing Act applies in full in Puerto Rico. Puerto Rico's local civil rights laws extend protection to four additional classes beyond the seven federal protected classes. All four are directly relevant to the platform's algorithm audit obligations.

Protected Classes — Full List for Puerto Rico

ClassSource
Relevance to Platform AlgorithmsRace, color, national origin, religion, sex, familial status, disability
Federal Fair Housing ActStandard algorithm audit scope. HUD 2023 AI guidance on disparate impact applies.
Political ideology (ideología política)Puerto Rico local law
Any ranking or recommendation model that correlates with known political geography in PR must be tested.Social condition (condición social)
Puerto Rico local lawGeographic proxies (neighborhood name, ZIP code, property type) must be tested for social condition disparate impact.
Sexual orientation and gender identityPuerto Rico local law
No proxy variables in ranking models that could correlate with these classes.Source of income
Puerto Rico local lawRental search filters may not exclude Section 8 voucher holders. No listing may state 'no housing vouchers.'

Platform Obligations

* Conduct annual disparate impact audit of all search ranking, recommendation, and matching algorithms.

* Geographic proxy testing: any use of ZIP code, neighborhood name, school district, or property type in ranking must be tested against ALL Puerto Rico protected classes including political ideology and social condition.

* Listing content screening: automated and manual review of listing text for discriminatory language. Flag any listing stating preferences based on any protected class.

* Rental filters: do not allow filtering by source of income. Remove any filter that could screen out housing voucher holders.

* Advertising audiences: do not use protected class attributes or their proxies to define ad targeting audiences for Puerto Rico.

sections.title: ADA Web Accessibility
sections.contentHtml:
* Standard: WCAG 2.1 Level AA — confirmed by federal courts as the applicable standard.

* Bilingual requirement: Puerto Rico platforms must be fully accessible in both English and Spanish. Screen-reader compatibility must be verified in both languages.

* Property photo alt-text: descriptive alt-text required on all listing images. Format: '[Feature] with [detail] and [detail]' — e.g. 'Ocean-view terrace with tile flooring and aluminum railings'.

* Interactive maps: keyboard-only operability required for all property and neighborhood maps.

* Skip-navigation links: required for screen reader users.

* Annual audit: at least one manual screen-reader audit per year in addition to automated testing. Audit must cover Spanish-language interface.

sections.title: Email Marketing — CAN-SPAM
sections.contentHtml:
* CAN-SPAM applies fully in Puerto Rico as a U.S. territory.

* Opt-out model: prior opt-in not required for commercial emails — but an unsubscribe mechanism must be present in every commercial message.

* Unsubscribe: must be honored within 10 business days of the opt-out request.

* Listing alerts: apply the primary purpose test. If the subject line implies commercial promotion, all CAN-SPAM rules apply.

* Transactional messages (VOW registration confirmations, booking receipts): largely exempt if no misleading routing information.

* Civil penalty: $53,088 per separate violating email (2026 FTC-adjusted rate).

* Bilingual: listing alert and marketing email templates should be available in both English and Spanish given Puerto Rico's bilingual market.

sections.title: Data Privacy
sections.contentHtml:
Puerto Rico does not have its own comprehensive data protection law. Federal frameworks govern platform data obligations.

* FTC Act Section 5: unfair or deceptive data practices prohibited. Applies to all platform data handling in Puerto Rico.

* COPPA: applies for users under 13. Platform must not knowingly collect personal data from minors.

* GLBA: if the platform processes mortgage pre-qualification or lending data, GLBA financial privacy rules apply.

* Out-of-Puerto-Rico users: if the platform has users in California, Colorado, Virginia, Texas, Florida, Connecticut, or Washington, the applicable state privacy laws (CCPA, CPA, VCDPA, TDPSA, FDBR, CTDPA, My Health MY Data Act) apply to those users regardless of the platform's Puerto Rico domicile.

* CCPA/GPC signal: if any California users are served, the Global Privacy Control (GPC) signal must be honored as a valid opt-out as of January 1, 2026.

* Privacy notice: publish a clear privacy policy disclosing what data is collected, how it is used, and how users can request access, correction, or deletion.

* Marketing emails: obtain consent or ensure the processing meets a valid legal basis before using registration data for marketing purposes.
